From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The **United Ladies' Tailors' Trade Union** was a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") representing [tailors](/wiki/Tailor "Tailor") in London. Most of its members were Jewish, although all tailors were eligible to join.

The union was founded in 1907 as the **London Ladies' Tailors', Machinists' and Pressers' Trade Union**, but by 1911 had only 388 members. Given that the large majority of its members were Jewish, union business was conducted in [Yiddish](/wiki/Yiddish "Yiddish"). In 1912, it grew enormously to have 3,235 members, thereafter remaining at roughly this level.[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1)

In 1919, [Jacob Fine](/wiki/Jacob_Fine "Jacob Fine") became general secretary of the union, and organised mergers with as many small ladies' tailors unions as possible. On the urging of the [Trades Union Congress](/wiki/Trades_Union_Congress "Trades Union Congress"), it discussed a possible merger with the [Amalgamated Society of Tailors and Tailoresses](/wiki/Amalgamated_Society_of_Tailors_and_Tailoresses "Amalgamated Society of Tailors and Tailoresses") and the [Tailors and Garment Workers Trade Union](/wiki/Tailors_and_Garment_Workers_Trade_Union "Tailors and Garment Workers Trade Union"), but it decided against joining either union. However, in 1939 it did amalgamate with the [National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Tailors_and_Garment_Workers "National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers").[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1)

## General Secretaries

* Salve Josephs
* 1919: [Jacob Fine](/wiki/Jacob_Fine "Jacob Fine")
